criteria.name: Required sections present
criteria.autoCheckMethod: Check for: Problem Statement, Proposed Solution, Acceptance Criteria, Out of Scope sections

criteria.name: Cross-references valid
criteria.description: Verify file paths mentioned in proposal exist on disk
criteria.autoCheckMethod: Verify any file paths mentioned in the proposal exist on disk

criteria.name: Acceptance criteria defined
criteria.description: Check for checkbox items or numbered criteria
criteria.autoCheckMethod: Check for `- [ ]` checkbox items or numbered criteria list

criteria.name: Prerequisites documented
criteria.description: Check for prerequisites or dependencies section
criteria.autoCheckMethod: Check for prerequisites/dependencies section if applicable

criteria.name: No internal contradictions
criteria.description: Verify solution addresses stated problem without contradictions
criteria.autoCheckMethod: Verify solution addresses the stated problem; out-of-scope items not duplicated in solution

criteria.name: Solution detail sufficient for implementation
criteria.autoCheckMethod: Check for named files, APIs, data structures, or implementation steps (not just high-level prose)

criteria.name: Alternatives considered
criteria.description: Check for alternatives or tradeoffs section
criteria.autoCheckMethod: Check for alternatives/tradeoffs section documenting at least one rejected approach

criteria.name: Impact assessment present
criteria.description: Check for impact, risk, or effort section
criteria.autoCheckMethod: Check for impact/risk/effort section covering scope, risk, and estimated effort

criteria.name: Implementation criteria match solution
criteria.autoCheckMethod: Cross-reference AC items against the proposed solution — each AC should map to a solution element

criteria.name: Edge cases and error handling addressed
criteria.description: Check for error handling or failure mode sections
criteria.autoCheckMethod: Check for error handling, edge case, or failure mode sections

criteria.name: Proposal self-contained
criteria.description: Check that external references are explained inline
criteria.autoCheckMethod: Check if external references (URLs, other repos, tools) are explained inline without requiring context

criteria.name: Writing clear and unambiguous
criteria.description: Evaluate prose for vague or ambiguous language
criteria.autoCheckMethod: Evaluate prose for vague language ("should work", "might need", "probably"), undefined terms, or ambiguous scope

criteria.name: Technical feasibility
criteria.description: Assess whether the proposed solution is technically achievable
criteria.autoCheckMethod: Assess whether the proposed solution is technically achievable. Check for: technical complexity and risk factors (novel tech, unproven approaches, scaling concerns), dependency availability and compatibility (external libraries, APIs, services), scope clarity and implementation feasibility (can the solution be built as described?), resource/effort proportionality (is the effort reasonable for the value?). Present any feasibility concerns with evidence.

criteria.name: Test coverage proportionate
criteria.description: Check that testing strategy is proportionate to scope
criteria.autoCheckMethod: When scope is non-trivial (multiple files, refactoring, new modules), check that the proposal mentions a testing strategy or includes test-related acceptance criteria. For simple single-file changes, testing mention is preferred but not required. Report scope assessment vs testing mentions with evidence.

criteria.name: Diagrams verified
criteria.description: Verify referenced diagram files exist on disk
criteria.autoCheckMethod: If `**Diagrams:**` field lists file paths (not "None"), verify each referenced file exists on disk. Report missing files as fail. If field is "None" or absent, skip this check (not an error).

criteria.name: Path Analysis present
criteria.description: Check for Path Analysis section in proposal
criteria.autoCheckMethod: Check for `## Path Analysis` section in proposal document. If missing, suggest: "Consider running `/paths #N` to identify scenario paths before PRD conversion." This is non-blocking — the review continues regardless of whether Path Analysis exists. If present, no suggestion displayed.

criteria.name: Screen coverage (UI proposals)
criteria.description: Check for screen specs or mockups in UI-related proposals
criteria.autoCheckMethod: If the proposal mentions screens, UI, forms, or user interface elements, check for `## Screen Specs` or `## Mockups` sections. If screens are mentioned but no specs/mockups are referenced, flag. If the proposal is CLI-only, API-only, or has no UI references, skip this check entirely.

subjective.name: Scope appropriate
subjective.question: Is the scope appropriate — neither too broad nor too narrow?

options.label: Appropriate
options.description: Scope is well-bounded, achievable, and addresses the core problem

options.label: Needs adjustment
options.description: Slightly too broad or missing a key aspect

options.label: Problematic
options.description: Scope is too large, too vague, or misses the core problem

subjective.partialReviewNote: Partial reviews are valid — if user declines to answer, record as '⊘ Skipped' and continue.

_meta.note: Proposal review criteria for /review-proposal. Extracted from inline command spec table.
